creator: jcfyecrayz@liamekaens.com
text:
(In reply to Jan Beich from comment #1)
The vc-intrinsics package that is installed is up to date, so that is different than bug 259900 where an older version of the package is installed in PREFIX, and we're trying to build a newer version.  Continuing along this line of thinking, however, that is why I tried deleting any (older) installed package of intel-graphics-compiler (as mentioned in comment 0).  As I said, that didn't make a difference.

However, I tried deleting vc-intrinsics and rebuilding / reinstalling it.  I did not expect that to help since the vc-intrinsics port itself did not change at all, but building intel-graphics-compiler did succeed after that.

vc-instrics-llvm12-s20211110 was built and installed on this system on Nov 30.  llvm12-12.0.1_6 was installed at that time.  Today llvm12-12.0.1_7 is installed.  The PORTREVISION=7 for llvm12 added the BW_WASM option - on by default and built that way on this system.  That adds the WebAssembly backend.  Could it be that the update to llvm12 that added the WebAssembly broke the vc-intrinsics-llvm12 lib that was built at the time?  If so, bumping PORTREVISION for vc-intrinsics when llvm12 was updated would have fixed this issue.  I would not have expected the addition of another backend to llvm12 (or perhaps just a no-change rebuild of the llvm12 lib that vc-intrinsic is dependent on) to cause a problem with vc-intrinsics.  But maybe that's what happened?  I'm not sure of any good way to tell for sure without doing a lot of forensics that probably is not worth the effort.
